pages.title: About - Paul Waugh
pages.meta_description: Labour and Co-operative MP for Rochdale
pages.text_excerpt: About - Paul Waugh Skip to main content Paul Waugh Labour and Co-operative MP for Rochdale Join Get Involved Donate News Surgeries In Parliament Useful Info Find out more Testimonials Acceptable Behaviour Policy Newsletter Sign Up Contact Privacy Notice More Menu About Paul Waugh MP Paul Waugh has been the Member of Parliament for Rochdale since July 2024. Born at Birch Hill Hospital in Wardle, Paul was raised on a Council estate in Spotland and went to Oulder Hill Community School before moving on to University in Oxford and Cardiff. Prior to becoming an MP, Paul was a journalist working at HuffPost UK, PoliticsHome, Evening Standard and The Independent. He also worked for the i newspaper and presented the Week in Westminster for the BBC. Paul is a passionate Rochdale AFC fan and longstanding season ticket holder. Rochdale is Paul’s home town and he is determined to get the best for it and the people who live here. Paul Waugh Contact details Address: 20 St Marys Gate, Rochdale OL16 1DZ Phone: 01706 391 482 Address: 20 St Marys Gate, Rochdale OL16 1DZ Phone: 01706 391 482 '> View on Google Maps Email: paul.waugh.mp@parliament.uk Further details Promoted by Tom Besford on behalf of Paul Waugh both of 89-91 Oldham Road, Rochdale OL16 5QR ©2026 Copyright Labour All rights reserved.
